hero.bio: Software engineer working on applied AI: multi-agent workflows, RAG, LLMOps, and the full-stack products around them. I care about shipping things that actually work in production.

about.subtitle: Engineer focused on applied AI, multi-agent systems, and production full-stack products.
about.paragraph: I mix CS fundamentals and competitive programming with product engineering. Right now I'm mostly working on {{a}}, {{b}}, {{c}}, and production RAG systems.

now.subtitle: A snapshot of what I'm building, learning, and reading this month.

items.title: Building
items.text: A multi-agent eval harness on LangGraph and Langfuse. Traces, judge models, and regression gates for agent workflows.

items.title: Learning
items.text: Hybrid retrieval, cross-encoder reranking, and structured RAG over enterprise knowledge graphs.

items.title: Reading
items.text: Papers on tool-use agents, LLM-as-judge reliability, and inference-time compute scaling.

portfolio.subtitle: From competitive CS to shipping AI agents in production.

education.institution: International Institute of Information Technology, Naya Raipur

education.degree: Agentic AI in Action: From Concepts to Real-World Impact

highlights: Led design of a Payroll Intelligence Agent with multi-agent workflows and eval loops. Cut median latency by 38% vs. the single-prompt baseline and lifted answer accuracy by 22% on the golden eval set.
Wired Langfuse tracing into every graph node. Trace coverage went to 100% of turns and on-call debugging dropped from hours to minutes.
Architected LangGraph orchestration with tool-calling, skill routing, guardrails, and RAG retrieval. Powers 4+ payroll skills behind one entry point.
Held p95 latency flat through query caching and zero-downtime online migrations, even as ingested policy volume doubled.
Shipped a PR-blocking regression suite. Judge models run over golden turns nightly and on demand, so prompt regressions get caught before merge.
Built 'Ray', an AI-powered fintech concierge on LLMs, LangGraph, and FastAPI. First-response time on auto-resolved tickets dropped from hours to seconds.
Automated a large share of merchant support workflows behind feature flags with human-in-the-loop review, so rollout stayed safe and gradual.
Wrote a Go eval engine that runs prompt suites concurrently. About 10x faster than the previous sequential runner, which unlocked daily prompt iteration.
Shipped multi-agent ticket resolution with context-aware processing and dynamic priority scoring. Urgent tickets jump the queue automatically.
Built secure REST APIs in Java and Spring Boot with role-based auth, serving 20+ downstream services across enterprise modules.
Shipped real-time gRPC notification services for critical alerts. Cross-service event latency went from seconds to sub-100ms.
Extended enterprise modules in C# and ASP.NET using patterns that got adopted across 3 product lines.
Built a reusable React and TypeScript component library (30+ components). Standardized internal UX and cut new-screen build time by about 40%.

skills.subtitle: Languages, frameworks, AI, infrastructure. Composed for shipping reliable products.

curated.title: Payroll Intelligence Agent
curated.description: Multi-agent copilot for enterprise payroll. Routes intents, calls tools, retrieves policy with RAG, and gates every prompt change through a judged eval set. Cut median latency by 38% and lifted accuracy 22% on the golden set.

curated.title: Ray — Fintech AI Concierge
curated.description: LLM-powered merchant support that turns hours-long tickets into second-level answers. Multi-agent orchestration in Python, Go eval engine that runs 10x faster than the sequential runner, rollout gated by feature flags and human review.

services.subtitle: From AI agents to backend platforms. Practical engineering that moves the needle.

testimonials.subtitle: Feedback from founders, CTOs, and product leads I've worked with across Europe, Asia, and the Americas.

items.name: Lukas Vogel
items.quote: Amulya rebuilt our agent orchestration from scratch. Latency dropped by half and our hallucination rate is finally under control. He owns the problem end to end.

items.name: Sofia Marchetti
items.quote: We hired Amulya to ship a RAG pipeline in four weeks. He delivered in three, with eval dashboards, traces, and a clean handoff to my team.

items.name: Hiroshi Tanaka
items.quote: Rare combination of deep ML intuition and production discipline. Our LLM eval infra is the most reliable piece of the stack now.

items.name: Élodie Laurent
items.quote: Calm, fast, and unusually clear in writing. Amulya turned a vague AI feature brief into a shipped product our users actually understand.

items.name: Daniel O'Connor
items.quote: He plugged into our team in days, not weeks. Honest about trade-offs, ruthless about scope, and the code he leaves behind is maintainable.

bullets: Mentored 200+ students in programming and full-stack development projects by personally answering doubts and providing individualized guidance.
Reviewed 1000+ programming problems and scripts for potential errors.
Provided solutions to 2500+ doubts across technical courses and contests on the platform.
Mentored 30+ students personally by solving doubts on DSA, programming, and web development.
Built a GitHub repository of 10+ projects and 100+ assessments to evaluate candidates across concepts and skills.
Encouraged undergraduate students to start businesses based on their visions for a better future.
Conducted a stock trading competition that engaged 350+ youngsters worldwide to promote financial awareness.
Organized the Entrepreneurship Summit, which attracted 2600+ students and 20+ speakers.
Assisted in organizing events to engage young enthusiasts in learning basic electronics and clearing their doubts.
Built projects on IoT, 3D printing, electronic devices, sensors, and actuators.
